method_atom: Structure theory of an algebraic object equipped with a distinguished anti-endomorphism (an 'antipode'-type operator) together with a group of invertible 'grouplike' units: one derives a formula expressing a fixed power of the anti-endomorphism as conjugation by a distinguished pair of invertible elements, forcing that operator to have finite order modulo an inner automorphism, and uses the trace of the square of the operator as a sufficient numerical criterion for the object to decompose into a direct sum of simple components; a duality argument then links such a decomposability property to its dual counterpart.
object_atom: Weak Hopf algebras and their duals — specifically their grouplike elements, their antipode S, and the dynamical-twisting deformations of semisimple Hopf algebras.
brief_reason: The transferable method is the abstract structure theory (a Radford-type power-of-operator formula, a trace-of-square decomposability criterion, and a property/dual duality); the domain-specific object is weak Hopf algebras and their antipodes/grouplikes, with some unavoidable fusion since the antipode and (co)semisimplicity notions are intrinsic to Hopf-type structures.
